```
<type>file</type>
<content>1: import { join, relative, dirname, resolve } from "path";
2: import { existsSync, copyFileSync, mkdirSync, statSync, readFileSync, readdirSync, unlinkSync } from "fs";
3: import { getPaths } from "../config/paths.js";
4: import { sessionRepository } from "../sessions/repository.js";
5: import crypto from "crypto";
6: 
7: export type FileStatus = 
8:   | "clean"      // File hasn't changed
9:   | "modified"   // File modified by agent [M]
10:   | "new"        // New file created by agent [?]
11:   | "deleted"    // File deleted by agent [D]
12:   | "conflict";  // Conflict with original repo [!]
13: 
14: export interface FileChange {
15:   path: string;
16:   status: FileStatus;
17:   timestamp: Date;
18:   checksum?: string;
19:   size?: number;
20:   lastModified?: Date;
21: }
22: 
23: export interface ChangeSet {
24:   sessionId: string;
25:   files: FileChange[];
26:   syncedAt?: Date;
27:   hasConflicts: boolean;
28: }
29: 
30: export interface FileSyncState {
31:   sessionId: string;
32:   upstreamPath: string;
33:   checkoutPath: string;
34:   changes: Map<string, FileChange>;
35:   baselineChecksums: Map<string, string>; // Track original checksums at last sync
36:   lastSyncAt?: Date;
37: }
38: 
39: export class FileSyncService {
40:   private syncStates: Map<string, FileSyncState> = new Map();
41:   private pendingChanges: Map<string, FileChange[]> = new Map(); // Buffered changes for reconnects
42: 
43:   async initializeSession(
44:     sessionId: string,
45:     checkoutPath: string,
46:     upstreamPath?: string
47:   ): Promise<void> {
48:     // Get paths from session if not provided
49:     if (!upstreamPath || !checkoutPath) {
50:       const session = await sessionRepository.findById(sessionId);
51:       if (!session) {
52:         throw new Error(`Session ${sessionId} not found`);
53:       }
54:       
55:       upstreamPath = upstreamPath || session.upstreamPath;
56:       checkoutPath = checkoutPath || session.checkoutPath;
57:     }
58: 
59:     const syncState: FileSyncState = {
60:       sessionId,
61:       upstreamPath,
62:       checkoutPath,
63:       changes: new Map(),
64:       baselineChecksums: new Map(),
65:     };
66: 
67:     this.syncStates.set(sessionId, syncState);
68:     
69:     // Initialize empty pending changes buffer
70:     this.pendingChanges.set(sessionId, []);
71: 
72:     // Scan current state
73:     await this.scanSessionCheckout(sessionId);
74:   }
75: 
76:   async handleFileChanges(
77:     sessionId: string, 
78:     changes: Array<{ path: string; isNew?: boolean; deleted?: boolean }>
79:   ): Promise<FileChange[]> {
80:     const syncState = this.syncStates.get(sessionId);
81:     if (!syncState) {
82:       await this.initializeSession(sessionId, "", "");
83:     }
84: 
85:     const fileChanges: FileChange[] = [];
86: 
87:     for (const change of changes) {
88:       let status: FileStatus = "modified";
89:       
90:       if (change.deleted) {
91:         status = "deleted";
92:       } else if (change.isNew) {
93:         status = "new";
94:       } else {
95:         // Check if file exists in original repo
96:         const originalPath = join(syncState!.upstreamPath, change.path);
97:         const sessionPath = join(syncState!.checkoutPath, change.path);
98:         
99:         if (!existsSync(originalPath) && existsSync(sessionPath)) {
100:           status = "new";
101:         }
102:       }
103: 
104:       // Check for conflicts
105:       const conflictStatus = await this.checkConflict(
106:         sessionId, 
107:         change.path, 
108:         status
109:       );
110: 
111:       const fileChange: FileChange = {
112:         path: change.path,
113:         status: conflictStatus || status,
114:         timestamp: new Date(),
115:         ...(status !== "deleted" && await this.getFileInfo(sessionId, change.path)),
116:       };
117: 
118:       syncState!.changes.set(change.path, fileChange);
119:       fileChanges.push(fileChange);
120:     }
121: 
122:     // Sync changes to original repo
123:     await this.syncChangesToUpstream(sessionId, fileChanges);
124: 
125:     return fileChanges;
126:   }
127: 
128:   private async checkConflict(
129:     sessionId: string,
130:     filePath: string,
131:     agentStatus: FileStatus
132:   ): Promise<FileStatus | null> {
133:     const syncState = this.syncStates.get(sessionId);
134:     if (!syncState) return null;
135: 
136:     // Skip conflict check for new files
137:     if (agentStatus === "new") return null;
138: 
139:     const originalPath = join(syncState.upstreamPath, filePath);
140:     
141:     // If file doesn't exist in original, no conflict
142:     if (!existsSync(originalPath)) {
143:       return null;
144:     }
145: 
146:     // Get checksums
147:     const sessionPath = join(syncState.checkoutPath, filePath);
148:     
149:     if (!existsSync(sessionPath)) {
150:       // File was deleted in session but exists in original
151:       // Check if original was modified since last sync
152:       const originalChecksum = await this.calculateChecksum(originalPath);
153:       const baselineChecksum = syncState.baselineChecksums.get(filePath);
154:       
155:       if (baselineChecksum && originalChecksum !== baselineChecksum) {
156:         return "conflict";
157:       }
158:       return null;
159:     }
160: 
161:     // Compare checksums
162:     const originalChecksum = await this.calculateChecksum(originalPath);
163:     const sessionChecksum = await this.calculateChecksum(sessionPath);
164:     const baselineChecksum = syncState.baselineChecksums.get(filePath);
165: 
166:     // If original changed since last sync and agent also changed it, it's a conflict
167:     if (baselineChecksum && 
168:         originalChecksum !== baselineChecksum && 
169:         sessionChecksum !== baselineChecksum) {
170:       return "conflict";
171:     }
172: 
173:     return null;
174:   }
175: 
176:   private async syncChangesToUpstream(
177:     sessionId: string, 
178:     changes: FileChange[]
179:   ): Promise<void> {
180:     const syncState = this.syncStates.get(sessionId);
181:     if (!syncState) return;
182: 
183:     for (const change of changes) {
184:       if (change.status === "conflict") {
185:         // Don't sync conflicts - require manual resolution
186:         continue;
187:       }
188: 
189:       const sessionPath = join(syncState.checkoutPath, change.path);
190:       const originalPath = join(syncState.upstreamPath, change.path);
191: 
192:       try {
193:         if (change.status === "deleted") {
194:           if (existsSync(originalPath)) {
195:             unlinkSync(originalPath);
196:           }
197:           // Remove from baseline
198:           syncState.baselineChecksums.delete(change.path);
199:         } else if (change.status === "new" || change.status === "modified") {
200:           // Ensure directory exists
201:           const originalDir = dirname(originalPath);
202:           if (!existsSync(originalDir)) {
203:             mkdirSync(originalDir, { recursive: true });
204:           }
205: 
206:           // Copy file with permissions
207:           if (existsSync(sessionPath)) {
208:             copyFileSync(sessionPath, originalPath);
209:             
210:             // Preserve timestamps if available
211:             if (change.lastModified) {
212:               const fs = require("fs");
213:               fs.utimesSync(originalPath, change.lastModified, change.lastModified);
214:             }
215:             
216:             // Update baseline checksum
217:             const newChecksum = await this.calculateChecksum(sessionPath);
218:             syncState.baselineChecksums.set(change.path, newChecksum);
219:           }
220:         }
221:       } catch (error) {
222:         console.error(`Failed to sync ${change.path}:`, error);
223:       }
224:     }
225: 
226:     // Update last sync time
227:     syncState.lastSyncAt = new Date();
228:   }
229: 
230:   async manualPullFromOriginal(sessionId: string): Promise<FileChange[]> {
231:     const syncState = this.syncStates.get(sessionId);
232:     if (!syncState) {
233:       throw new Error(`Session ${sessionId} not initialized`);
234:     }
235: 
236:     const changes: FileChange[] = [];
237: 
238:     // Scan original repo for changes
239:     await this.scanDirectory(
240:       syncState.upstreamPath,
241:       syncState.upstreamPath,
242:       async (originalPath, relativePath) => {
243:         const sessionPath = join(syncState.checkoutPath, relativePath);
244:         
245:         // Check if file exists in session
246:         if (!existsSync(sessionPath)) {
247:           // File exists in original but not in session - copy it
248:           const sessionDir = dirname(sessionPath);
249:           if (!existsSync(sessionDir)) {
250:             mkdirSync(sessionDir, { recursive: true });
251:           }
252:           
253:           copyFileSync(originalPath, sessionPath);
254:           
255:           const fileChange: FileChange = {
256:             path: relativePath,
257:             status: "modified",
258:             timestamp: new Date(),
259:             ...await this.getFileInfo(sessionId, relativePath),
260:           };
261:           
262:           syncState.changes.set(relativePath, fileChange);
263:           changes.push(fileChange);
264:         } else {
265:           // File exists in both - check if different
266:           const originalChecksum = await this.calculateChecksum(originalPath);
267:           const sessionChecksum = await this.calculateChecksum(sessionPath);
268:           const baselineChecksum = syncState.baselineChecksums.get(relativePath);
269:           
270:           if (originalChecksum !== sessionChecksum) {
271:             // Check if session file is modified by agent (differs from baseline)
272:             const sessionModified = baselineChecksum && sessionChecksum !== baselineChecksum;
273:             // Check if original file is modified
274:             const originalModified = baselineChecksum && originalChecksum !== baselineChecksum;
275:             
276:             if (sessionModified && originalModified) {
277:               // Conflict! Both changed
278:               const conflictChange: FileChange = {
279:                 path: relativePath,
280:                 status: "conflict",
281:                 timestamp: new Date(),
282:               };
283:               
284:               syncState.changes.set(relativePath, conflictChange);
285:               changes.push(conflictChange);
286:             } else {
287:               // Only original changed or only session changed (not both)
288:               // Copy from original to session
289:               copyFileSync(originalPath, sessionPath);
290:               
291:               // Update baseline
292:               syncState.baselineChecksums.set(relativePath, originalChecksum);
293:               
294:               const fileChange: FileChange = {
295:                 path: relativePath,
296:                 status: "modified",
297:                 timestamp: new Date(),
298:                 ...await this.getFileInfo(sessionId, relativePath),
299:               };
300:               
301:               syncState.changes.set(relativePath, fileChange);
302:               changes.push(fileChange);
303:             }
304:           }
305:         }
306:       }
307:     );
308: 
309:     syncState.lastSyncAt = new Date();
310:     return changes;
311:   }
312: 
313:   async resolveConflict(
314:     sessionId: string,
315:     filePath: string,
316:     resolution: "session" | "original" | "merge"
317:   ): Promise<void> {
318:     const syncState = this.syncStates.get(sessionId);
319:     if (!syncState) {
320:       throw new Error(`Session ${sessionId} not initialized`);
321:     }
322: 
323:     const sessionPath = join(syncState.checkoutPath, filePath);
324:     const originalPath = join(syncState.upstreamPath, filePath);
325: 
326:     if (resolution === "session") {
327:       // Keep session version
328:       if (existsSync(sessionPath)) {
329:         const originalDir = dirname(originalPath);
330:         if (!existsSync(originalDir)) {
331:           mkdirSync(originalDir, { recursive: true });
332:         }
333:         copyFileSync(sessionPath, originalPath);
334:       }
335:     } else if (resolution === "original") {
336:       // Keep original version
337:       if (existsSync(originalPath)) {
338:         const sessionDir = dirname(sessionPath);
339:         if (!existsSync(sessionDir)) {
340:           mkdirSync(sessionDir, { recursive: true });
341:         }
342:         copyFileSync(originalPath, sessionPath);
343:       }
344:     }
345:     // "merge" would require a merge tool - not implemented yet
346: 
347:     // Update status to clean (no longer conflicting)
348:     const fileChange: FileChange = {
349:       path: filePath,
350:       status: "clean",
351:       timestamp: new Date(),
352:       ...await this.getFileInfo(sessionId, filePath),
353:     };
354: 
355:     syncState.changes.set(filePath, fileChange);
356:   }
357: 
358:   async getChangeSet(sessionId: string): Promise<ChangeSet> {
359:     const syncState = this.syncStates.get(sessionId);
360:     if (!syncState) {
361:       return {
362:         sessionId,
363:         files: [],
364:         hasConflicts: false,
365:       };
366:     }
367: 
368:     const files = Array.from(syncState.changes.values());
369:     const hasConflicts = files.some(f => f.status === "conflict");
370: 
371:     return {
372:       sessionId,
373:       files: files.filter(f => f.status !== "clean"),
374:       hasConflicts,
375:       syncedAt: syncState.lastSyncAt,
376:     };
377:   }
378: 
379:   async getFileStatus(sessionId: string, filePath: string): Promise<FileStatus> {
380:     const syncState = this.syncStates.get(sessionId);
381:     if (!syncState) return "clean";
382: 
383:     const change = syncState.changes.get(filePath);
384:     return change?.status || "clean";
385:   }
386: 
387:   async scanSessionCheckout(sessionId: string): Promise<void> {
388:     const syncState = this.syncStates.get(sessionId);
389:     if (!syncState) return;
390: 
391:     // First scan the original repo to establish baseline
392:     await this.scanDirectory(
393:       syncState.upstreamPath,
394:       syncState.upstreamPath,
395:       async (fullPath, relativePath) => {
396:         const checksum = await this.calculateChecksum(fullPath);
397:         syncState.baselineChecksums.set(relativePath, checksum);
398:       }
399:     );
400: 
401:     // Then scan session worktree
402:     await this.scanDirectory(
403:       syncState.checkoutPath,
404:       syncState.checkoutPath,
405:       async (fullPath, relativePath) => {
406:         const fileChange: FileChange = {
407:           path: relativePath,
408:           status: "clean",
409:           timestamp: new Date(),
410:           ...await this.getFileInfo(sessionId, relativePath),
411:         };
412: 
413:         syncState.changes.set(relativePath, fileChange);
414:       }
415:     );
416:   }
417: 
418:   private async scanDirectory(
419:     dirPath: string,
420:     basePath: string,
421:     callback: (fullPath: string, relativePath: string) => Promise<void>
422:   ): Promise<void> {
423:     if (!existsSync(dirPath)) return;
424: 
425:     const entries = readdirSync(dirPath, { withFileTypes: true });
426:     
427:     for (const entry of entries) {
428:       const fullPath = join(dirPath, entry.name);
429:       const relativePath = relative(basePath, fullPath);
430: 
431:       // Skip hidden files and directories
432:       if (entry.name.startsWith(".")) continue;
433: 
434:       if (entry.isDirectory()) {
435:         await this.scanDirectory(fullPath, basePath, callback);
436:       } else {
437:         await callback(fullPath, relativePath);
438:       }
439:     }
440:   }
441: 
442:   private async getFileInfo(sessionId: string, filePath: string): Promise<Partial<FileChange>> {
443:     const syncState = this.syncStates.get(sessionId);
444:     if (!syncState) return {};
445: 
446:     const sessionPath = join(syncState.checkoutPath, filePath);
447:     
448:     if (!existsSync(sessionPath)) {
449:       return {};
450:     }
451: 
452:     const stats = statSync(sessionPath);
453:     const checksum = await this.calculateChecksum(sessionPath);
454: 
455:     return {
456:       size: stats.size,
457:       lastModified: stats.mtime,
458:       checksum,
459:     };
460:   }
461: 
462:   private async calculateChecksum(filePath: string): Promise<string> {
463:     const content = readFileSync(filePath);
464:     return crypto.createHash("md5").update(content).digest("hex");
465:   }
466: 
467:   async bufferChangesForReconnect(
468:     sessionId: string, 
469:     changes: FileChange[]
470:   ): Promise<void> {
471:     const buffered = this.pendingChanges.get(sessionId) || [];
472:     this.pendingChanges.set(sessionId, [...buffered, ...changes]);
473:   }
474: 
475:   async getBufferedChanges(sessionId: string): Promise<FileChange[]> {
476:     return this.pendingChanges.get(sessionId) || [];
477:   }
478: 
479:   async clearBufferedChanges(sessionId: string): Promise<void> {
480:     this.pendingChanges.delete(sessionId);
481:   }
482: 
483:   async cleanupSession(sessionId: string): Promise<void> {
484:     this.syncStates.delete(sessionId);
485:     this.pendingChanges.delete(sessionId);
486:   }
487: }
488:
```

```
</content>
```

## Agent's Thoughts (2026-04-05 01:23:37)

The user wants a detailed summary of what we've done so far. Let me review the conversation:

1. We started with exploring a commit feature that would copy changes from fossil proxy repo to upstream and commit/push
2. We discovered the architecture was broken - the commit service was trying to commit in a directory that wasn't a repo
3. We created a full OpenSpec change "fix-commit-flow" with proposal, design, specs, and tasks
4. We implemented the fix which involved:
   - Renaming `checkoutPath` to `agentWorkspacePath` throughout the codebase
   - Adding new VCS methods: fossilUp, cleanCopyToUpstream, commitUpstream, pushUpstream
   - Rewriting the commit service to implement a 4-step flow
   - Updating tests and documentation
5. We completed all 57 tasks and archived the change
6. Then the user reported mimo-agent stopped working with WebSocket error
7. We debugged and found the issue: the agent token contained sessionId and projectId fields that weren't expected
8. We fixed the AgentTokenPayload interface and verification logic

Now I need to create a comprehensive summary for the next agent.
## Agent (2026-04-05 01:23:40)

---
#### Goal

The user is working on the MIMO platform (Minimal IDE for Modern Operations), a web-based platform for AI-assisted development with session-based worktrees, file synchronization, and integrated chat with AI agents. The immediate goals were:

1. **Fix the broken commit flow** - The commit button was not working because the system tried to commit in a directory without a repository
2. **Fix agent WebSocket connection** - The mimo-agent was failing to connect with "Expected 101 status code" error

## Instructions

- Follow the 4-step commit flow architecture: `fossil up` → clean copy → commit → push
- The session directory structure changed: `checkout/` renamed to `agent-workspace/`
- Old sessions need recreation due to breaking change in session.yaml structure
- Agent tokens may contain extra fields (sessionId, projectId) that must be handled

#### Discoveries

### Commit Flow Architecture
The platform has a specific commit flow that was broken:
- **agent-workspace/**: Plain working directory (not a repository) where agent makes edits
- **repo.fossil**: Fossil proxy that agent clones from and pushes to
- **upstream/**: Original repository (Git or Fossil) where commits must happen
- The correct flow: `fossil up` in agent-workspace → copy to upstream → commit in upstream → push to remote

##### Session Directory Structure (NEW)
```
sessions/<session-id>/
├── session.yaml          # Now uses agentWorkspacePath field
├── repo.fossil           # Fossil proxy for agent sync
├── upstream/             # Original repo clone
└── agent-workspace/      # Was checkout/ - agent working directory
```

### Token Payload Issue
Agent tokens contain optional `sessionId` and `projectId` fields that must be handled in `AgentTokenPayload` interface and `verifyAgentToken()` method.

## Accomplished

### ✅ fix-commit-flow Change (COMPLETED - 57/57 tasks)
**Status**: Archived to `openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-05-fix-commit-flow/`

**Completed work:**
- Renamed `checkoutPath` → `agentWorkspacePath` in Session/SessionData interfaces
- Updated session repository to create `agent-workspace/` directory
- Added backward compatibility for reading old sessions
- Added 4 new VCS methods: `fossilUp()`, `cleanCopyToUpstream()`, `commitUpstream()`, `pushUpstream()`
- Rewrote CommitService with 4-step commit flow
- Updated commit routes (removed old endpoints, simplified API)
- Updated SessionDetailPage and ProjectDetailPage components
- Updated FileSyncService with renamed paths
- Added comprehensive tests for new VCS methods
- Updated README with new directory structure and commit flow documentation
- Updated existing tests to use new field names

### ✅ Agent Token Fix (COMPLETED)
**Fixed**: `packages/mimo-platform/src/agents/service.ts`
- Extended `AgentTokenPayload` to include optional `sessionId` and `projectId`
- Updated `verifyAgentToken()` to extract all token fields
- Updated `generateAgentToken()` to support optional fields
- Updated `CreateAgentInput` interface

**Note**: All changes have been committed to git.

#### Relevant files / directories

### Core Implementation Files (Modified)
- `packages/mimo-platform/src/sessions/repository.ts` - Session interface and repository (renamed field, migration logic)
- `packages/mimo-platform/src/sessions/routes.tsx` - Session routes (updated path references)
- `packages/mimo-platform/src/sync/service.ts` - File sync service (renamed paths)
- `packages/mimo-platform/src/vcs/index.ts` - VCS module (added fossilUp, cleanCopyToUpstream, commitUpstream, pushUpstream)
- `packages/mimo-platform/src/commits/service.ts` - Commit service (completely rewritten)
- `packages/mimo-platform/src/commits/routes.ts` - Commit routes (simplified)
- `packages/mimo-platform/src/agents/service.ts` - Agent service (token payload fix)
- `packages/mimo-platform/src/components/SessionDetailPage.tsx` - Component interface update
- `packages/mimo-platform/src/components/ProjectDetailPage.tsx` - Component interface update

##### Test Files (Modified/Created)
- `packages/mimo-platform/test/vcs.test.ts` - Added tests for new VCS methods
- `packages/mimo-platform/test/commits.test.ts` - Created new test suite for commit service
- `packages/mimo-platform/test/session-bootstrap.test.ts` - Updated for renamed field
- `packages/mimo-platform/test/sessions.test.ts` - Updated for renamed field
- `packages/mimo-platform/test/agent-bootstrap-integration.test.ts` - Updated for renamed field
- `packages/mimo-platform/test/e2e.test.ts` - Updated to reflect new commit service API

##### Documentation
- `README.md` - Updated with new directory structure and commit flow documentation

##### OpenSpec Change Artifacts (Archived)
- `openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-05-fix-commit-flow/proposal.md`
- `openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-05-fix-commit-flow/design.md`
- `openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-05-fix-commit-flow/specs/commit-flow/spec.md`
- `openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-05-fix-commit-flow/specs/session-management/spec.md`
- `openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-05-fix-commit-flow/tasks.md`

## What's Next

The commit flow fix and agent token fix are complete. The next steps would be:

1. **Test the full workflow**: Create a new session, start mimo-agent, make changes, click Commit button
2. **Monitor for any remaining issues**: Check if agent connects properly and if commit flow works end-to-end
3. **Create new sessions**: Old sessions (created before this change) will not work due to `checkoutPath` → `agentWorkspacePath` rename

```
4: const JWT_SECRET = process.env.JWT_SECRET || "your-secret-key-change-in-production";
5: const AGENT_SECRET = new TextEncoder().encode(JWT_SECRET);
6: 
7: export interface AgentTokenPayload {
8:   agentId: string;
9:   sessionId?: string;
10:   projectId?: string;
11:   owner: string;
12: }
13: 
14: export interface CreateAgentInput {
15:   owner: string;
16:   sessionId?: string;
17:   projectId?: string;
18: }
19: 
20: export class AgentService {
21:   private activeConnections: Map<string, WebSocket> = new Map();
22:   private currentAcpRequest: Map<string, AbortController> = new Map();
23:   private agentWorkdirs: Map<string, string> = new Map();
24: 
25:   constructor(
26:     private repository: AgentRepository = agentRepository
27:   ) {}
28: 
29:   async createAgent(input: CreateAgentInput): Promise<Agent> {
30:     const agent = await this.repository.create({
31:       owner: input.owner,
32:     });
33: 
34:     const token = await this.generateAgentToken(agent, input.sessionId, input.projectId);
35:     await this.repository.update(agent.id, { token });
36:     
37:     return { ...agent, token };
38:   }
39: 
40:   async generateAgentToken(agent: Agent, sessionId?: string, projectId?: string): Promise<string> {
41:     const tokenPayload: any = {
42:       agentId: agent.id,
43:       owner: agent.owner,
44:     };
45:     
46:     if (sessionId) {
47:       tokenPayload.sessionId = sessionId;
48:     }
49:     if (projectId) {
50:       tokenPayload.projectId = projectId;
51:     }
52:     
53:     const token = await new SignJWT(tokenPayload)
54:       .setProtectedHeader({ alg: "HS256" })
55:       .setIssuedAt()
56:       .setExpirationTime("24h")
57:       .sign(AGENT_SECRET);
58: 
59:     return token;
60:   }
61: 
62:   async verifyAgentToken(token: string): Promise<AgentTokenPayload | null> {
63:     try {
64:       const { payload } = await jwtVerify(token, AGENT_SECRET);
65:       return {
66:         agentId: payload.agentId as string,
67:         sessionId: payload.sessionId as string | undefined,
68:         projectId: payload.projectId as string | undefined,
69:         owner: payload.owner as string,
70:       };
71:     } catch {
72:       return null;
73:     }
74:   }
75: 
76:   async handleAgentConnect(agentId: string, ws: WebSocket, workdir?: string): Promise<void> {
77:     const agent = await this.repository.findById(agentId);
78:     if (!agent) {
79:       ws.close(1008, "Agent not found");
80:       return;
81:     }
82: 
83:     this.activeConnections.set(agentId, ws);
84:     if (workdir) {
85:       this.agentWorkdirs.set(agentId, workdir);
86:     }
87:     await this.repository.updateStatus(agentId, "online");
88:     await this.repository.updateLastActivity(agentId);
89:   }
90: 
91:   getAgentWorkdir(agentId: string): string | undefined {
92:     return this.agentWorkdirs.get(agentId);
93:   }
94: 
95:   async handleAgentDisconnect(agentId: string): Promise<void> {
96:     this.activeConnections.delete(agentId);
97:     await this.repository.updateStatus(agentId, "offline");
98:   }
99: 
100:   async cancelCurrentRequest(sessionId: string): Promise<boolean> {
101:     const sessionRepo = await import("../sessions/repository.js");
102:     const session = await sessionRepo.sessionRepository.findById(sessionId);
103:     if (!session || !session.assignedAgentId) {
104:       return false;
105:     }
106: 
107:     const agent = await this.repository.findById(session.assignedAgentId);
108:     if (!agent || agent.status !== "online") {
109:       return false;
110:     }
111: 
112:     const controller = this.currentAcpRequest.get(session.assignedAgentId);
113:     if (controller) {
114:       controller.abort();
115:       this.currentAcpRequest.delete(session.assignedAgentId);
116:     }
117: 
118:     const ws = this.activeConnections.get(session.assignedAgentId);
119:     if (ws) {
120:       ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: "cancel_request" }));
121:     }
122: 
123:     return true;
124:   }
125: 
126:   async deleteAgent(agentId: string): Promise<boolean> {
127:     const agent = await this.repository.findById(agentId);
128:     if (!agent) return false;
129: 
130:     if (this.activeConnections.has(agentId)) {
131:       const ws = this.activeConnections.get(agentId);
132:       ws?.close(1000, "Agent deleted");
133:       this.activeConnections.delete(agentId);
134:     }
135: 
136:     this.currentAcpRequest.delete(agentId);
137:     await this.repository.delete(agentId);
138:     
139:     return true;
140:   }
141: 
142:   async getAgentStatus(agentId: string): Promise<Agent | null> {
143:     return this.repository.findById(agentId);
144:   }
145: 
146:   async listAgentsByOwner(owner: string): Promise<Agent[]> {
147:     return this.repository.findByOwner(owner);
148:   }
149: 
150:   async listAgentsByStatus(status: AgentStatus): Promise<Agent[]> {

(Showing lines 1-150 of 185. Use offset=151 to continue.)
</content>
```
## Agent's Thoughts (2026-04-05 01:24:49)

I see the issue. When verifying the token in the WebSocket upgrade handler, there's a potential issue. Let me check if the token is being parsed correctly. The error "Expected 101 status code" suggests the upgrade failed.

The issue might be that in the `fetch` handler (line 111), `verifyAgentToken` returns `null` if the token is invalid, but looking at line 111-114, if `payload` is `null`, it returns a 401 response instead of attempting the upgrade. But the upgrade itself should happen at line 116-127.

Let me check if there's an issue with how the `verifyAgentToken` is handling the token. The error might be in the token verification logic or the token might be expired.

Actually, looking at the agent token:
```
eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9.eyJhZ2VudElkIjoiOGQzNGJjODctZmFjMS00MmMwLWEwZWYtYmY2MWVhN2QyOGYzIiwic2Vzc2lvbklkIjoiZTA5YzdlYmUtOGQzYS00MjI2LThmOGUtNjFjYTYwZWZmYWEwIiwicHJvamVjdElkIjoiNzM4NzQ0NWQtMzNjMi00ZjFlLWI2Y2QtNmYzNTM5YWI0OTc5Iiwib3duZXIiOiJqb3ZhIiwiaWF0IjoxNzc1MjUwODcyLCJleHAiOjE3NzUzMzcyNzJ9.hTWtozTqj2lv4VVftc1ZUhJ8NDoRgkTDVjhXHCTtqzw
```

Let me decode this:
- Header: `eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9` = `{"alg":"HS256"}`


- Payload: `eyJhZ2VudElkIjoiOGQzNGJjODctZmFjMS00MmMwLWEwZWYtYmY2MWVhN2QyOGYzIiwic2Vzc2lvbklkIjoiZTA5YzdlYmUtOGQzYS00MjI2LThmOGUtNjFjYTYwZWZmYWEwIiwicHJvamVjdElkIjoiNzM4NzQ0NWQtMzNjMi00ZjFlLWI2Y2QtNmYzNTM5YWI0OTc5Iiwib3duZXIiOiJqb3ZhIiwiaWF0IjoxNzc1MjUwODcyLCJleHAiOjE3NzUzMzcyNzJ9`

I need to decode this base64 payload to see what's inside. The token structure looks standard JWT format with HS256 algorithm, and it contains agent metadata like ID, session ID, project ID, owner, and timestamps for issued-at and expiration. The payload decodes to show the token was issued on Saturday, April 5, 2026 at 4:34:32 AM and expires 24 hours later. Since the current time is already past the expiration, the token is invalid and needs regeneration. The server logs don't show any WebSocket errors either, suggesting the client isn't even attempting to connect.

I should investigate the client-side agent code to see why the connection isn't being initiated. Let me examine the main agent file to understand the connection logic. Looking at line 116-127, the upgrade flow is straightforward: parse the URL, extract the token from query parameters, verify it, then upgrade the request. The issue might be that the server isn't running on the expected port, or there's a mismatch in how the WebSocket endpoint is configured. The error suggests the upgrade itself is failing, which could mean the server is rejecting the connection before it even reaches the token verification step. The agent is attempting to connect on port 3000, but the platform server might be listening on a different port or not running at all. I need to verify the server's actual port configuration and ensure it's properly started before the agent can establish a WebSocket connection.

Looking at the URL parsing logic, `url.pathname.split('/')[2]` extracts the third segment from the path. If the agent connects to `/ws/agent?token=...`, then `split('/')` produces `['', 'ws', 'agent']` and index 2 is `'agent'`, so the type detection should work correctly. The issue is likely that the server isn't running or isn't accessible on the expected port. The token itself contains standard JWT claims - agent ID, session ID, project ID, owner, issued at timestamp, and expiration timestamp. When decoded, the expiration is set for 1775337272 seconds, which converts to Saturday, May 3, 2025 at 17:14:32 UTC. The token should still be valid, so the problem isn't authentication timing. I need to verify the platform server is actually running and listening on the correct port. The agent is attempting to connect to port 3000, but I should check if the server is actually running there. The WebSocket upgrade flow looks correct—when an agent connects, it extracts the token from query parameters, verifies it, and establishes the connection. The "Expected 101 status code" error suggests the server might not be properly configured to handle WebSocket upgrades, or there's a mismatch in how the upgrade is being handled between the HTTP request and WebSocket response. Looking at the code structure, there's a potential issue with the conditional logic around the upgrade process that could prevent successful WebSocket connections. a 404. That's correct behavior.

Let me check the actual logs from the server to see if the WebSocket upgrade is being attempted:

import { SignJWT } from "jose";

const agentId = "8d34bc87-fac1-42c0-a0ef-bf61ea7d28f3";
const sessionId = "e09c7ebe-8d3a-4226-8f8e-61ca60effaa0";
const projectId = "7387445d-33c2-4f1e-b6cd-6f3539ab4979";
const owner = "jova";
const JWT_SECRET = process.env.JWT_SECRET || "your-secret-key-change-in-production";
const AGENT_SECRET = new TextEncoder().encode(JWT_SECRET);

const tokenPayload: any = {
  agentId: agentId,
  owner: owner,
  sessionId: sessionId,
  projectId: projectId,
};

const token = await new SignJWT(tokenPayload)
  .setProtectedHeader({ alg: "HS256" })
  .setIssuedAt()
  .setExpirationTime("24h")
  .sign(AGENT_SECRET);

console.log(token);
EOF
bun run /tmp/gen-token.ts 2>&1

## Agent's Thoughts (2026-04-05 01:30:32)

The issue was that the token had expired. When a token expires, the JWT verification fails with a `JWTExpired` error. I've improved the error logging in `verifyAgentToken` so the server will now log the specific reason for token verification failures, making it easier to diagnose expired token issues in the future.

The solution is:
1. Get a fresh token from the platform (go to the agent page at `/agents/8d34bc87-fac1-42c0-a0ef-bf61ea7d28f3` and copy the current token)
2. Tokens are valid for 24 hours from creation
3. The server now logs the specific error when token verification fails, which will help diagnose issues

Try running the agent again with a fresh token.

````
<skill_content name="llm-vcs-session-saver">
# Skill: llm-vcs-session-saver

# LLM VCS Session Saver

Saves an LLM conversation session as a flat text file optimized for version control: one line per message, unique timestamped filename, no identifying information. Works with any VCS — Git, SVN, Mercurial, Perforce, and others.

---

## Output File Path

Save the session file to:

```
llm-sessions/YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS-XXXX-session.txt
```

- `YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS` — timestamp of when the session was saved (UTC)
- `XXXX` — 4-character random alphanumeric string to prevent collisions
- Do **not** include usernames, email addresses, or any identifying info in the filename or content

Example: `llm-sessions/2025-04-01-143022-k7qr-session.txt`

---

## Content Format

Each message must be written as a **single line** in this exact format:

```
[YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS] User: <message text>
[YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS] LLM: <message text>
```

### Rules

- **Chronological order** — messages appear in the order they occurred
- **Timestamps** — use `YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS` format (UTC preferred)
- **Speaker labels** — only `User:` and `LLM:` (no other labels or roles)
- **One line per message** — newlines within a message must be replaced with a space or `\n` literal so each message stays on one line
- **No summarizing** — reproduce messages fully and verbatim
- **No extra formatting** — no markdown, no section headers, no blank lines between messages
- **No metadata block** — do not prepend titles, model names, or session IDs

### Example Output

```
[2025-04-01 14:30:01] User: What is the capital of France?
[2025-04-01 14:30:03] LLM: The capital of France is Paris.
[2025-04-01 14:30:10] User: And what's the population?
[2025-04-01 14:30:12] LLM: Paris has a population of approximately 2.1 million in the city proper.
```

---

## Git Readiness

- The file can be written directly and committed with no post-processing
- One-line-per-message ensures Git diffs are clean and human-readable — each changed message shows as exactly one modified line
- The `llm-sessions/` directory should be committed as part of the repo (not gitignored)

---

## Commit Workflow

Every time the user asks to make a Git commit — for any reason, even if unrelated to sessions — run this skill first, then include the session file in the commit.

Steps to follow on every commit:

1. **Generate the session file** using the path format and content rules above
2. **Stage the session file** along with the user's other changes:
   ```bash
   git add llm-sessions/YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS-XXXX-session.txt
   git add <any other files the user wants to commit>
   ```
3. **Commit everything together**:
   ```bash
   git commit -m "<user's commit message>"
   ```

The session file should reflect the full conversation up to and including the commit request itself.

---

## Multi-Session Safety

- Filenames are unique by design (timestamp + random ID), so concurrent sessions will never overwrite each other
- No locking or coordination needed between sessions
- If two sessions start in the same second, the random suffix (`XXXX`) prevents collision

Base directory for this skill: file:///home/bit4bit/.config/opencode/skills/session-history
Relative paths in this skill (e.g., scripts/, reference/) are relative to this base directory.
Note: file list is sampled.

<skill_files>

</skill_files>
</skill_content>
````

```
7: interface RunningServer {
8:   port: number;

10:   repoPath: string;

13: export class FossilServerManager {
14:   private portsInUse: Set<number> = new Set();
15:   private runningServers: Map<string, RunningServer> = new Map();
16: 
17:   assignPort(): number | null {
18:     for (let port = PORT_RANGE_START; port <= PORT_RANGE_END; port++) {
19:       if (!this.portsInUse.has(port)) {
20:         this.portsInUse.add(port);
21:         return port;
22:       }
23:     }
24:     return null;
25:   }
26: 
27:   releasePort(port: number): void {
28:     this.portsInUse.delete(port);

30: 
31:   async startServer(sessionId: string, repoPath: string): Promise<{ port: number } | { error: string }> {
32:     // Check if server already running for this session
33:     if (this.runningServers.has(sessionId)) {
34:       const server = this.runningServers.get(sessionId)!;
35:       return { port: server.port };
36:     }
37: 
38:     // Check if repo exists
39:     if (!existsSync(repoPath)) {
40:       return { error: `Repository not found: ${repoPath}` };

44:     const port = this.assignPort();
45:     if (port === null) {
46:       return { error: "PORTS_EXHAUSTED" };

54:

58:     // Check if process started successfully

60:       this.releasePort(port);
61:       return { error: "Failed to start Fossil server" };
62:     }
63: 
64:     // Track running server

113:
```

The fossil server manager assigns ports starting from 8000, but it doesn't check if the port is actually available (not in use by another process). It only tracks ports it has assigned itself.

The issue is that port 8000 might be in use by another process. Let me check:

## Agent's Thoughts (2026-04-05 01:35:38)

Port 8000 is already in use by a fossil process (PID 1845257) from a previous session. The FossilServerManager is assigning port 8000 without checking if it's actually available on the system.

The issue is that `assignPort()` only checks the internal `portsInUse` Set, but doesn't verify if the port is actually free on the system. A previous server instance might have crashed or not cleaned up properly, leaving port 8000 occupied.

Let me kill the old fossil process and also improve the port allocation to check if ports are actually available.

  process: ChildProcess;
  repoPath: string;

  private runningServers: Map<string, RunningServer> = new Map();

newString: import { spawn, ChildProcess } from "child_process";
import { existsSync } from "fs";
import { createConnection } from "net";

const PORT_RANGE_START = 8000;
const PORT_RANGE_END = 9000;

interface RunningServer {
  port: number;
  process: ChildProcess;
  repoPath: string;
}

export class FossilServerManager {
  private portsInUse: Set<number> = new Set();
  private runningServers: Map<string, RunningServer> = new Map();

  private async isPortAvailable(port: number): Promise<boolean> {
    return new Promise((resolve) => {
      const socket = createConnection(port, "localhost");
      socket.on("connect", () => {
        socket.destroy();
        resolve(false); // Port is in use
      });
      socket.on("error", () => {
        resolve(true); // Port is available
      });
    });
  }

  async assignPort(): Promise<number | null> {
    for (let port = PORT_RANGE_START; port <= PORT_RANGE_END; port++) {
      if (!this.portsInUse.has(port)) {
        // Check if port is actually available on the system
        const available = await this.isPortAvailable(port);
        if (available) {
          this.portsInUse.add(port);
          return port;
        }
      }
    }
    return null;
  }

## Agent's Thoughts (2026-04-05 01:36:00)

Done. The fix now:
1. Checks if the port is actually available on the system before assigning it
2. If a port is in use (like 8000 was), it skips to the next available port

This ensures that even if a fossil server crashes and leaves a port in use, or if something else is using port 8000, the server will find an available port from the pool.
## Agent (2026-04-05 01:36:00)

Done! I've fixed the port assignment issue.

**The problem:** Port 8000 was already in use by a fossil process that didn't clean up properly. The old code was assigning ports based only on internal tracking, not checking if the port was actually available on the system.

**The fix:** Modified `fossil-server.ts` to actually check if a port is available before assigning it:

```typescript
private async isPortAvailable(port: number): Promise<boolean> {
  return new Promise((resolve) => {
    const socket = createConnection(port, "localhost");
    socket.on("connect", () => {
      socket.destroy();
      resolve(false); // Port is in use
    });
    socket.on("error", () => {
      resolve(true); // Port is available
    });
  });
}

async assignPort(): Promise<number | null> {
  for (let port = PORT_RANGE_START; port <= PORT_RANGE_END; port++) {
    if (!this.portsInUse.has(port)) {
      // Check if port is actually available on the system
      const available = await this.isPortAvailable(port);
      if (available) {
        this.portsInUse.add(port);
        return port;
      }
    }
  }
  return null;
}
```

Now when the agent connects, it will skip port 8000 (which was taken) and use the next available port (8001 or higher).

**To use it:** Restart the mimo-platform server and run the agent again - it should now get a working port.

```
<content>1: import { spawn, ChildProcess } from "child_process";
2: import { existsSync } from "fs";

4: 
5: const PORT_RANGE_START = 8000;
6: const PORT_RANGE_END = 9000;
7: 
8: interface RunningServer {
9:   port: number;
10:   process: ChildProcess;
11:   repoPath: string;
12: }
13: 
14: export class FossilServerManager {
15:   private portsInUse: Set<number> = new Set();
16:   private runningServers: Map<string, RunningServer> = new Map();
17: 
18:   private async isPortAvailable(port: number): Promise<boolean> {
19:     return new Promise((resolve) => {
20:       const socket = createConnection(port, "localhost");
21:       socket.on("connect", () => {
22:         socket.destroy();
23:         resolve(false); // Port is in use
24:       });
25:       socket.on("error", () => {
26:         resolve(true); // Port is available
27:       });
28:     });
29:   }
30: 
31:   async assignPort(): Promise<number | null> {
32:     for (let port = PORT_RANGE_START; port <= PORT_RANGE_END; port++) {
33:       if (!this.portsInUse.has(port)) {
34:         // Check if port is actually available on the system
35:         const available = await this.isPortAvailable(port);
36:         if (available) {
37:           this.portsInUse.add(port);
38:           return port;
39:         }
40:       }
41:     }
42:     return null;
43:   }
44: 
45:   releasePort(port: number): void {
46:     this.portsInUse.delete(port);
47:   }
48: 
49:   async startServer(sessionId: string, repoPath: string): Promise<{ port: number } | { error: string }> {
50:     // Check if server already running for this session
51:     if (this.runningServers.has(sessionId)) {
52:       const server = this.runningServers.get(sessionId)!;
53:       return { port: server.port };
54:     }
55: 
56:     // Check if repo exists
57:     if (!existsSync(repoPath)) {
58:       return { error: `Repository not found: ${repoPath}` };
59:     }
60: 
61:     // Assign port
62:     const port = await this.assignPort();
63:     if (port === null) {
64:       return { error: "PORTS_EXHAUSTED" };
65:     }
66: 
67:     // Start fossil server
68:     const proc = spawn("fossil", ["server", "--port", port.toString(), repoPath], {
69:       stdio: "pipe",
70:       detached: false,
71:     });
72: 
73:     // Wait for server to start (simple check: wait a bit)
74:     await new Promise((resolve) => setTimeout(resolve, 500));
75: 
76:     // Check if process started successfully
77:     if (proc.pid === undefined) {
78:       this.releasePort(port);
79:       return { error: "Failed to start Fossil server" };
80:     }
81: 
82:     // Track running server
83:     this.runningServers.set(sessionId, {
84:       port,
85:       process: proc,
86:       repoPath,
87:     });
88: 
89:     // Handle process exit
90:     proc.on("exit", () => {
91:       this.runningServers.delete(sessionId);
92:       this.releasePort(port);
93:     });
94: 
95:     return { port };
96:   }
97: 
98:   async stopServer(sessionId: string): Promise<void> {
99:     const server = this.runningServers.get(sessionId);
100:     if (!server) {
101:       return;
102:     }
103: 
104:     // Kill the process
105:     server.process.kill("SIGTERM");
106: 
107:     // Wait for process to exit
108:     await new Promise<void>((resolve) => {
109:       server.process.on("exit", () => {
110:         resolve();
111:       });
112:       // Force kill after 5 seconds
113:       setTimeout(() => {
114:         server.process.kill("SIGKILL");

116:       }, 5000);
117:     });
118: 
119:     // Cleanup
```

The fossil server is started with `fossil server --port PORT repoPath`. To run without authentication (allowing anonymous commits), I need to add the `--no-ssl-verify` flag... wait, that's not right. Let me check fossil documentation.

Actually, the issue is that fossil by default requires authentication. The `--public` flag makes the server run in public mode where anyone can push/commit.

Let me add that flag to the spawn command.

## Agent's Thoughts (2026-04-05 01:45:21)

The user is pointing out that `--public` mode won't work for their use case. They need authentication, but they want it to be automatic/credential-based rather than interactive login.

Let me understand what they're asking:

1. When a session is created, generate fossil credentials (username/password)
2. These credentials should have "dev" capabilities (read/write)
3. Share these credentials with the mimo-agent when the session is created
4. The mimo-agent uses these credentials to clone and get read/write access

This is a different approach - instead of anonymous access, they want pre-provisioned credentials that are automatically shared.

## Agent (2026-04-05 01:45:23)

I see the issue. `--public` mode gives anonymous read access, but doesn't solve the write/commit problem properly. You need the agent to have credentials, but you want those credentials to be automatically provisioned and shared, not interactive.

Let me think through this architecture...

```
Current Flow (broken):
┌─────────────┐     fossil clone      ┌─────────────────┐
│  mimo-agent  │ ─────────────────────▶ │  fossil server   │
│  (no creds)  │                        │  (needs login)   │
└─────────────┘                        └─────────────────┘
        │                                      │
        │  clone fails - needs auth           │
        ◀─────────────────────────────────────┘

Proposed Flow:
┌─────────────┐     session creation    ┌──────────────┐
│   platform   │ ───────────────────────▶ │   session    │
│              │   generate fossil creds  │              │
│              │   (user: agent-{id})     │   repo.fossil │
│              │   (pass: {random})        │              │
└─────────────┘                           └──────────────┘
        │                                        │
        │  send credentials                      │  embed user
        │  in session_ready                      │  with DEV caps
        ▼                                        │
┌─────────────┐                                  │
│  mimo-agent  │ ◀───────────────────────────────┘
│              │     fossil server has
│              │     pre-provisioned user
└─────────────┘
        │
        │  fossil clone http://user:pass@localhost:port/
        ▼
  [authenticated clone with write access]
```

This is actually a clean model. The platform provisions a "service account" in fossil for each session, gives it to the agent, and the agent uses HTTP basic auth in the URL.

```
Usage: fossil user SUBCOMMAND ...  ?-R|--repository REPO?

Run various subcommands on users of the open repository or of
the repository identified by the -R or --repository option.

  fossil user capabilities USERNAME ?STRING?

       Query or set the capabilities for user USERNAME

  fossil user contact USERNAME ?CONTACT-INFO?

       Query or set contact information for user USERNAME

  fossil user default ?OPTIONS? ?USERNAME?

       Query or set the default user.  The default user is the
       user for command-line interaction.  If USERNAME is an
       empty string, then the default user is unset from the
       repository and will subsequently be determined by the -U
       command-line option or by environment variables
       FOSSIL_USER, USER, LOGNAME, or USERNAME, in that order.
       OPTIONS:

           -v|--verbose        Show how the default user is computed

  fossil user list | ls

       List all users known to the repository

  fossil user new ?USERNAME? ?CONTACT-INFO? ?PASSWORD?

       Create a new user in the repository.  Users can never be
       deleted.  They can be denied all access but they must continue
       to exist in the database.

  fossil user password USERNAME ?PASSWORD?

       Change the web access password for a user.
```

## Agent's Thoughts (2026-04-05 01:46:03)

Perfect! So fossil does support user management with capabilities. The letters are:
- `a` = Admin
- `c` = Clone (read)
- `d` = Delete
- `e` = Email
- `g` = Graft
- `h` = History
- `i` = Check-in (write)
- `j` = Project info
- `k` = Wiki
- `m` = Moderator
- `o` = Read-only ops
- `p` = Password
- `q` = Ticket
- `r` = Read
- `s` = Setup
- `w` = Write (for check-ins)
- `x` = Private
- `z` = Zip download

So for the agent, we need capabilities like `ci` or `ciw` (clone, check-in, write).

Now, thinking through the architecture...

The platform needs to:
1. When creating a session, generate fossil credentials (user: agent-{sessionId}, pass: random)
2. Add this user to the repo.fossil with appropriate capabilities
3. Pass these credentials to the agent via the session_ready message

The agent needs to:
1. Use the credentials in the fossil clone URL: `http://user:pass@localhost:port/`
2. Store them for subsequent push/pull operations

```
89:         if (!filename) return;
90: 
91:         // Skip hidden files and common ignore patterns
92:         if (
93:           filename.startsWith(".") ||
94:           filename.includes("/.") ||
95:           filename.includes("node_modules") ||
96:           filename.includes("__pycache__") ||
97:           filename.endsWith(".tmp") ||
98:           filename.endsWith("~")
99:         ) {
100:           return;
101:         }
102: 
103:         const change: FileChange = {
104:           path: filename,
105:           isNew: eventType === "rename",
106:           deleted: false,
107:         };
108: 
109:         const changes = this.pendingChanges.get(sessionId) || [];
110:         changes.push(change);
111:         this.pendingChanges.set(sessionId, changes);
112: 
113:         const existingTimeout = this.changeTimeouts.get(sessionId);
114:         if (existingTimeout) {
115:           clearTimeout(existingTimeout);
116:         }
117: 
118:         const timeout = setTimeout(() => {
119:           this.flushPendingChanges(sessionId);
120:         }, 500);
121:         this.changeTimeouts.set(sessionId, timeout);
122:       }
123:     );
124: 
125:     const session = this.sessions.get(sessionId);
126:     if (session) {
127:       session.fileWatcher = watcher;
128:     }
129:   }
130: 
131:   private flushPendingChanges(sessionId: string): void {
132:     const changes = this.pendingChanges.get(sessionId);
133:     if (!changes || changes.length === 0) return;
134: 
135:     this.pendingChanges.set(sessionId, []);
136:     this.changeTimeouts.delete(sessionId);
137: 
138:     // Deduplicate changes
139:     const uniqueChanges = new Map<string, FileChange>();
140:     for (const change of changes) {
141:       uniqueChanges.set(change.path, change);
142:     }
143: 
144:     this.callbacks.onFileChange(
145:       sessionId,
146:       Array.from(uniqueChanges.values())
147:     );
148:   }
149: 
150:   terminateSession(sessionId: string): void {
151:     const session = this.sessions.get(sessionId);
152:     if (!session) return;
153: 
154:     if (session.acpProcess) {
155:       console.log(`[mimo-agent] Killing ACP process for ${sessionId}`);
156:       session.acpProcess.kill("SIGTERM");
157:     }
158: 
159:     if (session.fileWatcher) {
160:       session.fileWatcher.close();
161:     }
162: 
163:     // Clear any pending changes
164:     const timeout = this.changeTimeouts.get(sessionId);
165:     if (timeout) {
166:       clearTimeout(timeout);
167:       this.changeTimeouts.delete(sessionId);
168:     }
169:     this.pendingChanges.delete(sessionId);
170: 
171:     this.sessions.delete(sessionId);
172:   }
173: 
174:   terminateAll(): void {
175:     for (const sessionId of this.sessions.keys()) {
176:       this.terminateSession(sessionId);
177:     }
178:   }
179: }
```

````
<skill_content name="openspec-ff-change">
# Skill: openspec-ff-change

Fast-forward through artifact creation - generate everything needed to start implementation in one go.

**Input**: The user's request should include a change name (kebab-case) OR a description of what they want to build.

**Steps**

1. **If no clear input provided, ask what they want to build**

   Use the **AskUserQuestion tool** (open-ended, no preset options) to ask:
   > "What change do you want to work on? Describe what you want to build or fix."

   From their description, derive a kebab-case name (e.g., "add user authentication" → `add-user-auth`).

   **IMPORTANT**: Do NOT proceed without understanding what the user wants to build.

2. **Create the change directory**
   ```bash
   openspec new change "<name>"
   ```
   This creates a scaffolded change at `openspec/changes/<name>/`.

3. **Get the artifact build order**
   ```bash
   openspec status --change "<name>" --json
   ```
   Parse the JSON to get:
   - `applyRequires`: array of artifact IDs needed before implementation (e.g., `["tasks"]`)
   - `artifacts`: list of all artifacts with their status and dependencies

4. **Create artifacts in sequence until apply-ready**

   Use the **TodoWrite tool** to track progress through the artifacts.

   Loop through artifacts in dependency order (artifacts with no pending dependencies first):

   a. **For each artifact that is `ready` (dependencies satisfied)**:
      - Get instructions:
        ```bash
        openspec instructions <artifact-id> --change "<name>" --json
        ```
      - The instructions JSON includes:
        - `context`: Project background (constraints for you - do NOT include in output)
        - `rules`: Artifact-specific rules (constraints for you - do NOT include in output)
        - `template`: The structure to use for your output file
        - `instruction`: Schema-specific guidance for this artifact type
        - `outputPath`: Where to write the artifact
        - `dependencies`: Completed artifacts to read for context
      - Read any completed dependency files for context
      - Create the artifact file using `template` as the structure
      - Apply `context` and `rules` as constraints - but do NOT copy them into the file
      - Show brief progress: "✓ Created <artifact-id>"

   b. **Continue until all `applyRequires` artifacts are complete**
      - After creating each artifact, re-run `openspec status --change "<name>" --json`
      - Check if every artifact ID in `applyRequires` has `status: "done"` in the artifacts array
      - Stop when all `applyRequires` artifacts are done

   c. **If an artifact requires user input** (unclear context):
      - Use **AskUserQuestion tool** to clarify
      - Then continue with creation

5. **Show final status**
   ```bash
   openspec status --change "<name>"
   ```

**Output**

After completing all artifacts, summarize:
- Change name and location
- List of artifacts created with brief descriptions
- What's ready: "All artifacts created! Ready for implementation."
- Prompt: "Run `/opsx:apply` or ask me to implement to start working on the tasks."

**Artifact Creation Guidelines**

- Follow the `instruction` field from `openspec instructions` for each artifact type
- The schema defines what each artifact should contain - follow it
- Read dependency artifacts for context before creating new ones
- Use `template` as the structure for your output file - fill in its sections
- **IMPORTANT**: `context` and `rules` are constraints for YOU, not content for the file
  - Do NOT copy `<context>`, `<rules>`, `<project_context>` blocks into the artifact
  - These guide what you write, but should never appear in the output

**Guardrails**
- Create ALL artifacts needed for implementation (as defined by schema's `apply.requires`)
- Always read dependency artifacts before creating a new one
- If context is critically unclear, ask the user - but prefer making reasonable decisions to keep momentum
- If a change with that name already exists, suggest continuing that change instead
- Verify each artifact file exists after writing before proceeding to next

Base directory for this skill: file:///home/bit4bit/src/mimo/.opencode/skills/openspec-ff-change
Relative paths in this skill (e.g., scripts/, reference/) are relative to this base directory.
Note: file list is sampled.

</skill_content>
````

oldString:   async createFossilRepo(repoPath: string): Promise<VCSResult> {

      success: result.success,

newString:   async createFossilRepo(repoPath: string): Promise<VCSResult> {
    const result = await this.execCommand(["fossil", "init", repoPath]);
    return {
      success: result.success,
      output: result.output,
      error: result.error || undefined,
    };
  }

  async createFossilUser(
    repoPath: string,
    username: string,
    password: string
  ): Promise<VCSResult> {
    // Create user with DEV capabilities (d - develop, i - check-in, o - check-out)
    const result = await this.execCommand([
      "fossil",
      "user",
      "new",
      username,
      username,
      password,
      "-R",
      repoPath,
    ]);

    if (result.success) {
      // Set capabilities to DEV (d=develop, i=check-in, o=check-out)
      const capResult = await this.execCommand([
        "fossil",
        "user",
        "capabilities",
        username,
        "dio",
        "-R",
        repoPath,
      ]);

      return {
        success: capResult.success,
        output: capResult.output,
        error: capResult.error || undefined,
      };
    }

    return {
      success: false,
      output: result.output,
      error: result.error || "Failed to create user",
    };
  }

oldString:     // Step 2: Import to fossil proxy (repo.fossil)
    const fossilPath = `${session.upstreamPath}/../repo.fossil`;
    const importResult = await vcs.importToFossil(
      session.upstreamPath,
      project.repoType,
      fossilPath
    );

      await sessionRepository.delete(projectId, session.id);
      return c.text(`Failed to import to fossil: ${importResult.error}`, 500);
    }
newString:     // Step 2: Import to fossil proxy (repo.fossil)
    const fossilPath = `${session.upstreamPath}/../repo.fossil`;
    const importResult = await vcs.importToFossil(
      session.upstreamPath,
      project.repoType,
      fossilPath
    );
    
    if (!importResult.success) {
      await sessionRepository.delete(projectId, session.id);
      return c.text(`Failed to import to fossil: ${importResult.error}`, 500);
    }

    // Step 3: Create fossil user for agent access
    const fossilUser = `agent-${session.id.slice(0, 8)}`;
    const fossilPassword = crypto.randomUUID().replace(/-/g, "").slice(0, 16);
    const userResult = await vcs.createFossilUser(fossilPath, fossilUser, fossilPassword);
    
    if (!userResult.success) {
      console.error("[session] Failed to create fossil user:", userResult.error);
      // Non-fatal - session can still work, just log the error
    }
    
    // Save credentials to session
    await sessionRepository.update(session.id, {
      fossilUser,
      fossilPassword,
    });

```
19: class MimoAgent {
20:   private ws: WebSocket | null = null;
21:   private config: AgentConfig;
22:   private sessionManager: SessionManager;
23:   private acpClients: Map<string, AcpClient> = new Map();
24:   private reconnectAttempts = 0;
25:   private maxReconnectAttempts = 5;
26:   private reconnectDelay = 1000;
27:   private provider: OpencodeProvider;
28: 
29:   constructor() {
30:     this.config = this.parseArgs();
31:     this.sessionManager = new SessionManager(this.config.workDir, {
32:       onFileChange: (sessionId, changes) => {
33:         this.send({
34:           type: "file_changed",
35:           sessionId,
36:           files: changes,
37:           timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
38:         });
39:       },
40:       onSessionError: (sessionId, error) => {
41:         this.send({
42:           type: "session_error",
43:           sessionId,
44:           error,
45:           timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
46:         });
47:       },
48:     });
49:     this.provider = new OpencodeProvider();
50:   }
51: 
52:   private parseArgs(): AgentConfig {
53:     const args = process.argv.slice(2);
54:     const config: Partial<AgentConfig> = {};
55: 
56:     for (let i = 0; i < args.length; i++) {
57:       const arg = args[i];
58:       if (arg === "--token" && i + 1 < args.length) {
59:         config.token = args[++i];
60:       } else if (arg === "--platform" && i + 1 < args.length) {
61:         config.platform = args[++i];
62:       } else if (arg === "--workdir" && i + 1 < args.length) {
63:         config.workDir = args[++i];
64:       }
65:     }
66: 
67:     if (!config.token) {
68:       throw new Error("Missing required argument: --token");
69:     }
70:     if (!config.platform) {
71:       throw new Error("Missing required argument: --platform");
72:     }
73: 
74:     if (!config.workDir) {
75:       config.workDir = process.cwd();
76:     }
77: 
78:     return config as AgentConfig;
79:   }
80: 
81:   async start(): Promise<void> {
82:     console.log("[mimo-agent] Starting...");
83:     console.log(`[mimo-agent] Platform: ${this.config.platform}`);
84:     console.log(`[mimo-agent] WorkDir: ${this.config.workDir}`);
85: 
86:     await this.connect();
87:     this.setupShutdownHandlers();
88:   }
89: 
90:   private async connect(): Promise<void> {
91:     const wsUrl = `${this.config.platform}?token=${this.config.token}`;
92: 
93:     return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
94:       console.log(`[mimo-agent] Connecting to ${this.config.platform}...`);
95: 
96:       this.ws = new WebSocket(wsUrl);
97: 
98:       this.ws.on("open", () => {
99:         console.log("[mimo-agent] Connected to platform");
100:         this.reconnectAttempts = 0;
101: 
102:         this.send({
103:           type: "agent_ready",
104:           workdir: this.config.workDir,
105:           timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
106:         });
107: 
108:         resolve();
109:       });
110: 
111:       this.ws.on("message", (data: Buffer) => {
112:         try {
113:           const message = JSON.parse(data.toString());
114:           this.handleMessage(message);
115:         } catch (error) {
116:           console.error("[mimo-agent] Failed to parse message:", error);
117:         }
118:       });
119: 
120:       this.ws.on("close", () => {
121:         console.log("[mimo-agent] Disconnected");
122:         this.handleDisconnect();
123:       });
124: 
125:       this.ws.on("error", (error: Error) => {
126:         console.error("[mimo-agent] WebSocket error:", error.message);
127:         reject(error);
128:       });
129: 
130:       setTimeout(() => {
131:         if (this.ws?.readyState !== WebSocket.OPEN) {
132:           reject(new Error("Connection timeout"));
133:         }
134:       }, 10000);
135:     });
136:   }
137: 
138:   private handleMessage(message: any): void {
139:     switch (message.type) {
140:       case "ping":
141:         this.send({ type: "pong" });
142:         break;
143: 
144:       case "session_ready":
145:         this.handleSessionReady(message);
146:         break;
147: 
148:       case "acp_request":
149:         this.handleAcpRequest(message);
150:         break;
151: 
152:       case "cancel_request":
153:         this.handleCancelRequest(message);
154:         break;
155: 
156:       case "file_sync_request":
157:         this.handleFileSyncRequest(message);
158:         break;
159: 
160:       case "user_message":
161:         this.handleUserMessage(message);
162:         break;
163: 
164:       case "set_model":
165:         this.handleSetModel(message);
166:         break;
167: 
168:       case "set_mode":
169:         this.handleSetMode(message);
170:         break;
171: 
172:       case "request_state":
173:         this.handleRequestState(message);
174:         break;
175: 
176:       default:
177:         console.log("[mimo-agent] Unknown message type:", message.type);
178:     }
179:   }
180: 
181:   private async handleSessionReady(message: any): Promise<void> {
182:     const { platformUrl, sessions } = message;
183: 
184:     if (!sessions || sessions.length === 0) {
185:       console.log("[mimo-agent] No sessions assigned");
186:       return;
187:     }
188: 
189:     console.log(`[mimo-agent] Received ${sessions.length} session(s)`);
190:     const sessionIds: string[] = [];
191: 
192:     for (const session of sessions) {
193:       const { sessionId, port } = session;
194: 
195:       try {
196:         const checkoutPath = join(this.config.workDir, sessionId);
197:         const platformHost = platformUrl
198:           .replace(/^https?:\/\//, "")
199:           .replace(/\/+$/, "");
200:         const fossilUrl = `http://${platformHost.split(":")[0]}:${port}/`;
201: 
202:         // Setup checkout directory
203:         await this.setupCheckout(sessionId, checkoutPath, fossilUrl);
204: 
205:         // Create session
206:         const sessionInfo = await this.sessionManager.createSession(
207:           sessionId,
208:           fossilUrl
209:         );
210: 
211:         // Spawn ACP process
212:         this.spawnAcpProcess(sessionInfo);
213: 
214:         sessionIds.push(sessionId);
215:         console.log(`[mimo-agent] Session ${sessionId} ready`);
216:       } catch (error) {
217:         console.error(
218:           `[mimo-agent] Failed to setup session ${sessionId}:`,
219:           error
220:         );
221:         this.send({
222:           type: "session_error",
223:           sessionId,
224:           error: error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error),
225:           timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
226:         });
227:       }
228:     }
229: 
230:     this.send({
231:       type: "agent_sessions_ready",
232:       sessionIds,
233:       timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
234:     });
235:   }
236: 
237:   private async setupCheckout(
238:     sessionId: string,
239:     checkoutPath: string,
240:     fossilUrl: string
241:   ): Promise<void> {
242:     const repoPath = join(checkoutPath, "..", `${sessionId}.fossil`);
243: 
244:     if (existsSync(repoPath)) {
245:       console.log(`[mimo-agent]   Fossil repo exists, opening`);
246:       if (!existsSync(checkoutPath)) {
247:         mkdirSync(checkoutPath, { recursive: true });
248:       }
249:       try {
250:         execSync(`fossil open ${repoPath}`, { cwd: checkoutPath, stdio: "pipe" });
251:       } catch {
252:         // Already open or error, continue
253:       }
254:     } else if (existsSync(join(checkoutPath, ".fossil"))) {
255:       console.log(`[mimo-agent]   Checkout exists, ensuring open`);
256:       try {
257:         execSync(`fossil open`, { cwd: checkoutPath, stdio: "pipe" });
258:       } catch {
259:         // Already open or error, continue
260:       }
261:     } else {
262:       console.log(`[mimo-agent]   Cloning from fossil`);
263:       execSync(`fossil clone ${fossilUrl} ${repoPath}`, { stdio: "pipe" });
264:       if (!existsSync(checkoutPath)) {
265:         mkdirSync(checkoutPath, { recursive: true });
266:       }
267:       execSync(`fossil open ${repoPath}`, { cwd: checkoutPath, stdio: "pipe" });
268:     }
269:   }
270: 
271:   private spawnAcpProcess(session: any): void {
272:     const sessionInfo = this.sessionManager.getSession(session.sessionId);
273:     if (!sessionInfo) return;
274: 
275:     // Terminate existing
276:     if (sessionInfo.acpProcess) {
277:       console.log(
278:         `[mimo-agent] Terminating existing ACP for ${session.sessionId}`
279:       );
280:       sessionInfo.acpProcess.kill();
281:     }
282: 
283:     const spawnResult = this.provider.spawn(sessionInfo.checkoutPath);
284:     const process = spawnResult.process;
285:     this.sessionManager.setSessionAcpProcess(session.sessionId, process);
286: 
287:     // Create ACP client
288:     const acpClient = new AcpClient(
289:       this.provider,
290:       session.sessionId,
291:       {
292:         onThoughtStart: (sessionId) => {
293:           this.send({
294:             type: "thought_start",
295:             sessionId,
296:             timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
297:           });
298:         },
299:         onThoughtChunk: (sessionId, content) => {
300:           this.send({
301:             type: "thought_chunk",
302:             sessionId,
303:             content,
304:             timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
305:           });
306:         },
307:         onThoughtEnd: (sessionId) => {
308:           this.send({
309:             type: "thought_end",
310:             sessionId,
311:             timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
312:           });
313:         },
314:         onMessageChunk: (sessionId, content) => {
315:           this.send({
316:             type: "message_chunk",
317:             sessionId,
318:             content,
319:             timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
320:           });
321:         },
322:         onUsageUpdate: (sessionId, usage) => {
323:           this.send({
324:             type: "usage_update",
325:             sessionId,
326:             usage,
327:             timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
328:           });
329:         },
330:         onGenericUpdate: (sessionId, content) => {
331:           this.send({
332:             type: "acp_response",
333:             sessionId,
334:             content,
335:             timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
336:           });
337:         },
338:       }
339:     );
340: 
341:     // Initialize ACP client
342:     acpClient
343:       .initialize(
344:         sessionInfo.checkoutPath,
345:         toWebWritable(spawnResult.stdin),
346:         toWebReadable(spawnResult.stdout)
347:       )
348:       .then(() => {
349:         console.log(`[mimo-agent] ACP client ready for ${session.sessionId}`);
350:         this.acpClients.set(session.sessionId, acpClient);
351: 
352:         // Update session state
353:         this.sessionManager.setSessionState(
354:           session.sessionId,
355:           acpClient.modelState,
356:           acpClient.modeState
357:         );
358: 
359:         // Send session initialized
360:         this.send({
361:           type: "session_initialized",
362:           sessionId: session.sessionId,
363:           modelState: acpClient.modelState,
364:           modeState: acpClient.modeState,
365:           timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
366:         });
367:       })
368:       .catch((err) => {
369:         console.error(
370:           `[mimo-agent] ACP init error for ${session.sessionId}:`,
371:           err
372:         );
373:       });
374: 
375:     // Handle process events
376:     process.stderr?.on("data", (data: Buffer) => {
377:       console.error(
378:         `[mimo-agent] ACP stderr (${session.sessionId}):`,
379:         data.toString()
380:       );
381:     });
382: 
383:     process.on("close", (code: number | null) => {
384:       console.log(
385:         `[mimo-agent] ACP exited for ${session.sessionId} with code ${code}`
386:       );
387:       this.acpClients.delete(session.sessionId);
388:       this.sessionManager.setSessionAcpProcess(session.sessionId, null);
389:     });
390: 
391:     process.on("error", (err: Error) => {
392:       console.error(
393:         `[mimo-agent] ACP process error for ${session.sessionId}:`,
394:         err.message
395:       );
396:       this.send({
397:         type: "session_error",
398:         sessionId: session.sessionId,
399:         error: `ACP process error: ${err.message}`,
400:         timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
401:       });
402:     });
403:   }
404: 
405:   private handleAcpRequest(message: any): void {
406:     const sessionId = message.sessionId;
407:     if (!sessionId) {
408:       console.log("[mimo-agent] No sessionId in acp_request");
409:       return;
410:     }
411: 
412:     const session = this.sessionManager.getSession(sessionId);
413:     if (!session) {
414:       console.log(`[mimo-agent] Unknown session ${sessionId}`);
415:       return;
416:     }
417: 
418:     console.log(`[mimo-agent] Restarting ACP for session ${sessionId}`);
419:     this.spawnAcpProcess(session);
420:   }
421: 
422:   private handleCancelRequest(message: any): void {
423:     const sessionId = message.sessionId;
424:     if (!sessionId) {
425:       console.log("[mimo-agent] No sessionId in cancel_request");
426:       return;
427:     }
428: 
429:     const session = this.sessionManager.getSession(sessionId);
430:     if (!session) {
431:       console.log(`[mimo-agent] Unknown session ${sessionId}`);
432:       return;
433:     }
434: 
435:     if (session.acpProcess) {
436:       console.log(`[mimo-agent] Cancelling ACP for ${sessionId}`);
437:       session.acpProcess.kill("SIGTERM");
438:       this.sessionManager.setSessionAcpProcess(sessionId, null);
439:       this.acpClients.delete(sessionId);
440: 
441:       this.send({
442:         type: "acp_cancelled",
443:         sessionId,
444:         timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
445:       });
446:     }
447:   }
448: 
449:   private handleFileSyncRequest(message: any): void {
450:     const sessionId = message.sessionId;
451:     console.log(
452:       `[mimo-agent] File sync for session ${sessionId || "unknown"}`
453:     );
454:   }
455: 
456:   private handleUserMessage(message: any): void {
457:     const sessionId = message.sessionId;
458:     const content = message.content;
459: 
460:     if (!sessionId) {
461:       console.log("[mimo-agent] No sessionId in user_message");
462:       return;
463:     }
464: 
465:     const acpClient = this.acpClients.get(sessionId);

467:       console.log(`[mimo-agent] No ACP client for session ${sessionId}`);
468:       this.send({

471:         error: `No ACP connection for session: ${sessionId}`,
472:         timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
473:       });
474:       return;
475:     }
476: 
477:     console.log(`[mimo-agent] Sending prompt for session ${sessionId}`);
478:     acpClient
479:       .prompt(content)
480:       .then(() => {
481:         console.log(`[mimo-agent] Prompt completed for ${sessionId}`);
482:       })
483:       .catch((err) => {
484:         console.error(`[mimo-agent] Prompt error:`, err);
485:         this.send({
486:           type: "error_response",
487:           sessionId,
488:           error: `ACP prompt error: ${err.message}`,
489:           timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
490:         });
491:       });
492:   }
493: 
494:   private async handleSetModel(message: any): Promise<void> {
495:     const { sessionId, modelId } = message;
496:     console.log(`[mimo-agent] Set model for ${sessionId}: ${modelId}`);
497: 
498:     const acpClient = this.acpClients.get(sessionId);

500:       console.log(`[mimo-agent] No ACP client for session ${sessionId}`);
501:       return;
502:     }
503: 
504:     try {

507:       this.send({

530:     const acpClient = this.acpClients.get(sessionId);

558:   private handleRequestState(message: any): void {
559:     const { sessionId } = message;
560:     const acpClient = this.acpClients.get(sessionId);
561: 
562:     if (!acpClient) {
563:       console.log(`[mimo-agent] Unknown session ${sessionId} in request_state`);
```
